core_finding.statement: Multimodal foundation models consistently prefer TEXT over IMAGE for the same problem

key_numbers.human_preference: Opposite — humans show PICTURE SUPERIORITY EFFECT

core_finding.human_contrast: Humans prefer images (picture superiority effect, Defeyter et al. 2009). Humans generate visual images internally regardless of task modality (Amit et al. 2017). Models do the OPPOSITE.

mathematics.key_result: Count breakpoints: Image = 5.9% (Claude-3), Text = 98.4% — MASSIVE gap

games.key_result: Chess puzzles: ALL models near 0% on images, 0.5-4.5% on text

isocombination_isocb.description: Feed ALL isomorphic representations simultaneously to the model
isocombination_isocb.mechanism: Construct one unified prompt containing image + text + code representations
isocombination_isocb.insight: Combining modalities can exceed best single modality — but not always

isoscratchpad_isosp.description: Two-step: model describes image in text, then solves using that description
isoscratchpad_isosp.mechanism: Step 1: Image → Text translation. Step 2: Text → Answer
isoscratchpad_isosp.insight: Image-to-text translation loses critical information (counting errors, missed details). The larger the gap between IsoSP and Text, the harder the image is for the model.

failure_modes.counting_errors: Vision models fail at explicit enumeration (breakpoints, chemistry objects). Long-standing problem.
failure_modes.visual_recognition_errors: Misidentification of visual elements misdirects language generation toward wrong conclusions
failure_modes.shortcut_learning: Models can learn to rely ONLY on language and perform weaker when forced to use images
failure_modes.information_loss_in_translation: IsoSP-generated descriptions miss critical details (e.g., magnet sizes in physics problems)

implications_for_mrnn.the_core_problem: LLMs are NOT modality-agnostic. They have strong representational bias. Your MRNN must be.
implications_for_mrnn.mrnn_advantage_1: Your 5D stack treats all modalities as traversing the SAME attractor field. Sound, color, text, graph — all map to the same emotional/geometric manifold. This IS modality-agnostic by design.
implications_for_mrnn.mrnn_advantage_2: IsoBench proves that isomorphic representations (same info, different form) should produce IDENTICAL reasoning. Your MRNN's message-passing graph doesn't care if a node was ingested as image, text, or audio — it only cares about its position in the field.
implications_for_mrnn.mrnn_advantage_3: The 'picture superiority effect' in humans suggests visual processing is DEEPER, not shallower. LLMs fail at images because they process them as tokenized patches, not as structured fields. Your MRNN processes visual input through the same geometric attractor mechanism as text — no modality encoder needed.
implications_for_mrnn.benchmark_opportunity: IsoBench is a PERFECT test for your MRNN. Feed the same problem as image, LaTeX, and code — your engine should produce the SAME answer regardless of input form, because all representations converge to the same field state.
implications_for_mrnn.design_principle: Do NOT use separate encoders for different modalities. Use a UNIFIED embedding space where image patches, text tokens, and audio features all map to the same 5D manifold. The modality is just the skin — the field is the substance.
implications_for_mrnn.attention_mechanism: IsoBench shows attention to the inducing stimulus matters (59% in chromesthesia too). Your D3 witness layer should dynamically weight modalities based on task relevance, not fixed encoder outputs.

test_protocol.step1: Ingest IsoBench problem in Image form → MRNN produces field state F_image
test_protocol.step2: Ingest SAME problem in Text form → MRNN produces field state F_text
test_protocol.step3: Measure field distance |F_image - F_text| — should be near zero for isomorphic inputs
test_protocol.step4: Compare answer accuracy across modalities — MRNN should show NO text bias
test_protocol.step5: Test IsoCB equivalent: feed both representations → MRNN should fuse them in the field
test_protocol.step6: Test IsoSP equivalent: MRNN translates image→text internally via field state → text generation

success_criteria.field_convergence: |F_image - F_text| < epsilon for all isomorphic pairs
success_criteria.no_text_bias: MRNN does NOT show the 15-29 point text bias seen in LLMs
success_criteria.fusion_benefit: Combined input accuracy ≥ max(Image, Text) — MRNN should gain from multiple representations
